title: Christmas Eve White Chocolate Cheesecake
ingredients: ¼ cup fine, dry chocolate cookie crumbs (use gluten-free if possible)
6 ounces good quality white chocolate (do not use chips)
2 packages (8 ounces each) Philadelphia cream cheese, room temperature
2 ounces good quality white chocolate (for garnish)
instructions: Sprinkle cookie crumbs in the bottom of a 10-inch springform pan.

Place 6 ounces white chocolate in a microwaveable bowl and heat on medium power for 30-60 seconds, stir, then heat again for 30 more seconds until melted and smooth. Be careful as white chocolate can overheat and turn granular.
Place cream cheese in a large mixing bowl and beat until smooth.
Slowly beat in 6 tbsp sugar until smooth.
Add eggs one at a time and mix well.
Add melted white chocolate and combine well.
Pour filling carefully into springform pan to avoid scattering crumbs.
Remove from oven and let cheesecake rest for 5 minutes. It will not appear set.
While the cheesecake is baking, whisk together the sour cream, ½ cup sugar, and vanilla paste. Let sit while sugar dissolves.
Slowly pour the sour cream mixture over the top of the cheesecake to avoid disturbing the cream cheese layer.
Remove and let cool at room temperature, then refrigerate for at least 6 hours or overnight.
To serve, place cheesecake slices on plates and top with hot fudge sauce, berries, and white chocolate shavings.
notes: Adapted from Pacific Northwest Palate Cookbook, 1989, by Susan Bradley.
Use Valentine's Favorite Hot fudge sauce for serving.
Must be refrigerated at least 6 hours or overnight before serving.
White chocolate can overheat easily and turn granular, so monitor carefully when melting.
